description:
Replace FnDef.doc: Option<String> and ModuleDef.moduledoc: Option<String> with a uniform `attrs: Vec<Attribute>` carrier.

## Why now

.31.4 needs to attach @spec to fns. .31.3 needs to attach @type to modules. The existing single-string `doc` field doesn't generalize. Migrating once now avoids churning every consumer twice.

## Changes

### src/ast.rs

```
pub enum Attribute {
    Doc(String),
    ModuleDoc(String),
    Spec(SpecDecl),        // populated by .31.4
    TypeAlias(TypeAliasDecl),  // populated by .31.3
}

pub struct FnDef {
    // remove: doc: Option<String>
    pub attrs: Vec<Attribute>,
    // ...
}

pub struct ModuleDef {
    // remove: moduledoc: Option<String>
    pub attrs: Vec<Attribute>,
    // ...
}
```

### src/parser.rs

- parse_attribute() returns Attribute, not (String, String)
- The current @doc-and-only-@doc whitelist (line ~222) widens to recognize the four kinds
- Spec/TypeAlias bodies are parsed via .31.1; for this ticket, accept only Doc/ModuleDoc and reject Spec/TypeAlias with "not yet implemented" — .31.3 and .31.4 enable them

### Consumers to migrate

- src/repl.rs: `?<name>` lookup uses fn.doc; switch to scanning fn.attrs for Attribute::Doc
- Test runner (.16): uses doc strings as test descriptions; same pattern

## Tests

- Existing @doc/@moduledoc parser tests pass with the new representation
- REPL `?<name>` golden tests for docs unchanged
- A fn with multiple @doc lines errors (or concatenates — match current behavior)
- Spec/TypeAlias attribute bodies error with a clear "not yet implemented in this build" diag
title: Generalize FnDef / ModuleDef attribute representation
